parsed_review.summary: CP00-833 is a Risk-B, descriptor-only Platform Extensibility "permission matrix contract/type-shape bridge" pack (40 units, RP27.P06.M01.S07-RP27.P06.M03.S04, upstream CP00-832 / next CP00-834). The claim is correctly scoped: manifest.json sets production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status=in_progress, implementation_layer=descriptor, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, and makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim (manifest.json:6-8,441-462). Static inspection of the five active implementation refs confirms internal consistency. The new descriptor (registry.js:2565) chains from PlatformExtensibilityCp832FixturePermissionMatrixBridgeDescriptor with authority_boundary claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; its Hermes packet emits_runtime_receipt=false (registry.js:1837), Claude packet is read_only=true / promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false (registry.js:2059), and closeout handoff routes to CP00-834 / RP27.P06.M03.S05 with runtime_opening_allowed=false (registry.js:2205). Coverage/descriptor/contract validators (validators.js:1423-1538) enforce unit/deliverable/phase/micro/title distributions against the live plan (deliverable 14/10/8/2/6=40, micro 14/22/4=40 — verified against manifest included_units and PERMISSION_ROWS slices at registry.js:420-443/1134-1138) and enforce descriptor_only, runtime_execution=false, no API key/webhook/workflow secrets, no real client data, no permission-decision detail or audit bodies, no permission/audit/product-state writes; createRow bakes in the full no-write attestation and freezes every row (registry.js:1296-1316). Tests assert errors===[] plus the runtime-closed fields and handoff (model.test.js:420-450), and the contract JSON ties it together with latest_pack=CP00-833, latest_projection descriptor, validation.valid=true, and an all-false no_write_attestation (contract:281,33441-33457). Authority boundary and CP178+ review hardening are satisfied: auth preflight passed with non-zero valid-JSON stdout, summary-only review is disallowed for Risk B and was not used, no forbidden tools, and no omitted active untracked files (readiness-report.json, validation-report.json). Hermes patterns appear only as deterministic descriptor-only review/evidence packets, not embedded product runtime. Two P3 provenance notes only: (1) the four downstream required closeout artifacts (command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present — only manifest.json exists — which is the expected validate->review->adjudicate->inspect->commit ordering, not a defect; and (2) as a read-only reviewer without Bash I verified validation would pass by static inspection but did not execute the npm commands and command-evidence.json is absent. No P0/P1/P2 issues; nothing blocks pack or this-CP goal closeout. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approval authority; human final approval for any enterprise-trust/runtime opening stays closed and outside this receipt.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts pending generation
findings.details: required_pack_artifacts lists manifest.json, command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, but only manifest.json currently exists in docs/closeout-packs/cp00-833/ (Glob confirms a single file). Pack status is in_progress and pack_level_claude_review.status is pending with empty valid_review_receipts and empty invalid_review_attempts (manifest.json:1501-1510). claude-review-result.json and adjudication.md are downstream of this review, and command-evidence.json/construction-inspection.json record validation and inspection runs. This matches the accepted closeout ordering observed for CP00-832 (validate -> Claude review -> adjudicate -> inspect -> commit), not an implementation defect, but closeout cannot finalize and production_ready cannot flip true until they exist.
findings.recommendation: Let the closeout harness generate and validate the four remaining required artifacts before marking CP00-833 closed; require command-evidence.json to show exit_code 0 for every listed validation command plus git diff --check, and require exactly one valid hardened read-only review receipt before construction-inspection.json sets pack_production_ready=true.

findings.title: Validation pass verified by static inspection only, not executed
findings.details: As a read-only reviewer without Bash, I confirmed the coverage/descriptor/contract validators (validators.js:1423-1538), the three CP00-833 tests (model.test.js:420-450), and scripts/validate-rp27-platform-extensibility-contract.mjs are internally consistent and would pass: the descriptor/contract/no_write_attestation values satisfy every assertion, PERMISSION_ROWS slices yield the exact 14/22/4 row titles, and the contract JSON markers match the generated projection. I did not observe actual execution of npm test, npm run validate, or the other listed validation commands, and command-evidence.json is absent, so the runtime pass/fail is asserted from source rather than observed.
findings.recommendation: Gate closeout on command-evidence.json capturing a successful run of all listed validation commands plus git diff --check; treat this static review as independent code inspection, not a substitute for executed validation evidence.
